## Project: Ubiquitous
**Repository:** https://github.com/APrettyCoolProgram/ubiquitous  
**Branch:** development  
**Target Framework:** .NET 10

---

## Task: Document TextGames Architecture

### Overview
Document the architecture and user interface specifications for TextGames application types.

### TextGames Types

#### 1. Basic Type
A simplified text adventure interface with minimal UI components.

**User Interface Components:**

- **Game Output Area (Left Panel)**
  - Scrollable text display showing game narrative
  - Black and white theme
  - Consolas mono font
  - Auto-scrolls to show latest output

- **Command History (Bottom Panel)**
  - Shows recent commands entered
  - Helps track game progress
  - Scrollable view

- **Command Input (Bottom)**
  - Text box for entering commands
  - ">" prompt indicator
  - SEND button + Enter key support
  - Up/Down arrow keys to recall previous commands

---

#### 2. Fancy Type
An enhanced text adventure interface with additional status tracking and styling capabilities.

**User Interface Components:**

- **Game Output Area (Left Panel)**
  - Scrollable text display showing game narrative
  - Dark, modern theme
  - Consolas font
  - Ability to show colored text
  - Ability to show flashing text
  - Auto-scrolls to show latest output

- **Status Panel (Left Panel)**
  - Real-time display of player's status, location, and other relevant information
  - Updates automatically after commands

- **Inventory Panel (Right Panel)**
  - Real-time display of player's inventory
  - Styled item cards
  - Updates automatically after commands

- **Command History (Bottom Panel)**
  - Shows recent commands entered
  - Helps track game progress
  - Scrollable view

- **Command Input (Bottom)**
  - Text box for entering commands
  - ">" prompt indicator
  - SEND button + Enter key support
  - Up/Down arrow keys to recall previous commands

---

## Notes
- Both types share common command input functionality
- Fancy type extends Basic type with additional visual features and information panels
- Command history is accessible via arrow keys in both types
- All panels support scrolling for content that exceeds viewport
